ddls:
CREATE TABLE "tweets" (
  "tweet_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each tweet */,
  "username" TEXT /* Username of the user who posted the tweet */,
  "datetime" TEXT /* Date and time when the tweet was posted */,
  "text" TEXT /* Content of the tweet */,
  "is_reply" INTEGER /* Whether the tweet is a reply to another tweet */,
  "is_retweet" INTEGER /* Whether the tweet is a retweet */,
  "nbr_favorite" INTEGER /* Number of favorites for the tweet */,
  "nbr_reply" INTEGER /* Number of replies to the tweet */,
  "nbr_retweet" INTEGER /* Number of retweets for the tweet */,
  "tweet_language" TEXT /* Language of the tweet */,
  "tweet_location" TEXT /* Location of the tweet (if available) */,
  "tweet_urls" TEXT /* List of URLs in the tweet */,
  "tweet_mentions" TEXT /* List of users mentioned in the tweet */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("tweet_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "username" TEXT /* Username of the user */,
  "name" TEXT /* Full name of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "user_location" TEXT /* Location of the user */,
  "user_language" TEXT /* Language of the user */,
  "user_timezone" TEXT /* Timezone of the user */,
  "user_followers" INTEGER /* Number of followers of the user */,
  "user_following" INTEGER /* Number of users the user is following */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "sentiments" (
  "sentiment_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each sentiment analysis result */,
  "tweet_id" INTEGER /* ID of the tweet being analyzed */,
  "sentiment_score" REAL /* Sentiment score of the tweet (between -1 and 1) */,
  "sentiment_category" TEXT /* Category of the sentiment (e.g., positive, negative, neutral) */,
  "sentiment_confidence" REAL /* Confidence of the sentiment analysis result (between 0 and 1) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("sentiment_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_sentiments_tweet_id FOREIGN KEY ("tweet_id") REFERENCES tweets ("tweet_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "hashtags" (
  "hashtag_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each hashtag */,
  "tweet_id" INTEGER /* ID of the tweet using the hashtag */,
  "hashtag" TEXT /* Hashtag used in the tweet */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("hashtag_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_hashtags_tweet_id FOREIGN KEY ("tweet_id") REFERENCES tweets ("tweet_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "tweet_categories" (
  "category_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each category */,
  "category_name" TEXT /* Name of the category */,
  "category_description" TEXT /* Description of the category */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("category_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "tweet_category_map" (
  "tweet_id" INTEGER /* ID of the tweet */,
  "category_id" INTEGER /* ID of the category the tweet belongs to */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("tweet_id", "category_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "engagement" (
  "engagement_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each engagement metric */,
  "tweet_id" INTEGER /* ID of the tweet */,
  "likes" INTEGER /* Number of likes for the tweet */,
  "replies" INTEGER /* Number of replies to the tweet */,
  "retweets" INTEGER /* Number of retweets for the tweet */,
  "shares" INTEGER /* Number of shares for the tweet */,
  "mentions" INTEGER /* Number of mentions for the tweet */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("engagement_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_engagement_tweet_id FOREIGN KEY ("tweet_id") REFERENCES tweets ("tweet_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "cleaned_tweets" (
  "cleaned_tweet_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each cleaned tweet */,
  "tweet_id" INTEGER /* ID of the original tweet */,
  "cleaned_text" TEXT /* Cleaned and preprocessed text of the tweet */,
  "stemmed_text" TEXT /* Stemmed text of the tweet */,
  "lemmatized_text" TEXT /* Lemmatized text of the tweet */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("cleaned_tweet_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_cleaned_tweets_tweet_id FOREIGN KEY ("tweet_id") REFERENCES tweets ("tweet_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "entities" (
  "entity_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each entity */,
  "tweet_id" INTEGER /* ID of the tweet */,
  "entity_name" TEXT /* Name of the entity */,
  "entity_type" TEXT /* Type of the entity (e.g., person, organization, location) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("entity_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_entities_tweet_id FOREIGN KEY ("tweet_id") REFERENCES tweets ("tweet_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "topics" (
  "topic_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each topic */,
  "tweet_id" INTEGER /* ID of the tweet */,
  "topic_name" TEXT /* Name of the topic */,
  "topic_score" REAL /* Score of the topic (between 0 and 1) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("topic_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_topics_tweet_id FOREIGN KEY ("tweet_id") REFERENCES tweets ("tweet_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "roles" (
  "role_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each role */,
  "role_name" TEXT /* Name of the role */,
  "role_description" TEXT /* Description of the role */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("role_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "user_roles" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user */,
  "role_id" INTEGER /* ID of the role assigned to the user */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id", "role_id")
)
